channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Anglican church gets four new bishops
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Church of Nigeria (Anglican Communion) has elected four new Anglican bishops for dioceses across the country. Learn about the new episcopal leaders.
Read More: https://punchng.com/anglican-church-gets-four-new-bishops/

item.title: Shields ready to continue talks for Price super fight
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Claressa Shields is ready to resume talks for a women's super fight against Lauren Price with promoter Dmitriy Salita remaining hopeful that a deal can be agreed.

item.title: Kano empowers 1,900 butchers with tools
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano State Government empowers 1,900 butchers with essential tools, reinforcing commitment to economic growth, food safety, and hygiene standards in meat p
Read More: https://punchng.com/kano-empowers-1900-butchers-with-tools/

item.title: Wallace charges up Open leaderboard with early birdie run
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Matt Wallace posted three birdies in the opening six holes of his second round at The Open, briefly lifting him to tied-second and within two of the lead.

item.title: Uganda drops case against women arrested for kissing in public
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ugandan prosecutors drop case against two women arrested for kissing under the harsh anti-gay law, sparking calls for repeal.
Read More: https://punchng.com/uganda-drops-case-against-women-arrested-for-kissing-in-public/

item.title: Why Fed Independence Is Hanging by a Thread
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Last August, President Trump made the unprecedented choice of moving to fire Fed governor Lisa Cook. The administration claimed she was being terminated with cause, citing an ongoing investigation in alleged mortgage fraud committed by Cook. The legal battle between Cook and the administration has b

item.title: NBA NEC insists on July 18 election as DSS releases service provider
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Executive Council (NEC) of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has resolved that the 2026 NBA National Officers and General... The post NBA NEC insists on July 18 election as DSS releases service provider appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Atiku Demands Independent Probe Into Habila’s Death, Calls For Umahi’s Suspension
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has called for an independent and transparent investigation into the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila, who reportedly died at the private residence of the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, in Uburu, Ebonyi Sta

item.title: Shipping through Strait of Hormuz slows as US-Iran conflict intensifies
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped to its lowest level in months as escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran continue to disrupt one of the world’s most critical energy corridors. According to shipping data reviewed by Reuters, only three commodity vessels transi

item.title: Bisola Aiyeola opens up on loyalty, friendship dynamics
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Bisola explained that although she doesn’t expect her friends to “inherit” her beefs, she thinks that it’s unfair for them to be friends with her ex or even date him.

item.title: Flooding Is a Public Health Emergency, Says Lagos Health Commissioner
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos State Commissioner for Health, Prof. Akin Abayomi, has described flooding as a major public health emergency, warning that its impact extends beyond damaged infrastructure to increased disease outbreaks, displacement and disruption of essential services. Abayomi made this known in a statem

item.title: 2026 World Cup: FIFA appoints referee for Argentina vs Spain final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: FIFA has appointed Slavko Vinčić to referee the 2026 World Cup Final between Spain and Argentina. Vinčić will be joined by assistant referees Tomaž Klančnik and Andraž Kovačič. Adham Makhadmeh of Jordan has been appointed as the fourth official for the match at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. The Wor

item.title: Transfer: Olise desperate to leave Bayern Munich for Real Madrid
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: France midfielder, Michael Olise, is desperate to leave Bayern Munich for Real Madrid this summer, according to L’Equipe. The 24-year-old is believed to have told his teammates at the 2026 World Cup, that he wants to join the LaLiga giants. Olise has reportedly been “gathering informatio

item.title: Nigeria’s ambassador-designate to Mali receives letters of credence
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ambassador-designate of Nigeria to the Republic of Mali, Dr. Geoffrey Ijomah David Chima, has received his Letters of Credence ahead of his diplomatic posting. The Letters of Credence were presented to Chima by the Chief of Protocol at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ambassador Babagana Wakil,

item.title: England missing final piece of jigsaw - Kane
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane says the team are "missing that final piece of the jigsaw" and need to "find a way to get better" following defeat to Argentina.

item.title: Suspected bandit arrested in Katsina with phones, motorcycle
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops from the 17 Brigade have arrested a man suspected to be involved in banditry during a routine operation in Danmusa Local Government Area of Katsina State. According to security sources, the suspect, identified as Lawal Sani, was arrested around 3:14 p.m. on July 15 after soldiers returning fr

item.title: ‘No Plateau community is occupied by Fulani people’ – MACBAN
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Plateau State chapter of the Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria, MACBAN, has reacted to allegations raised by a social critic and advocate, Emmanuel Jelan, that about 16 communities in Barkin Ladi Local Government Area of the state have been forcefully taken over and occupied by Fu

item.title: Sheffield United manager Wilder extends contract
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Chris Wilder, in his third spell as Sheffield United manager, extends his contract until the summer of 2028.

item.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The UK government said taking the firm into public hands would safeguard "a vital national capability".

item.title: Who is Slavko Vincic? Meet the referee for the 2026 World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The appointment also places the 46-year-old among an exclusive group, as he becomes only the 23rd referee in history to oversee a FIFA World Cup final. The post Who is Slavko Vincic? Meet the referee for the 2026 World Cup final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Police Arrest Father for Allegedly Burning 10-Year-Old Daughter for Bed-Wetting in Ondo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a man identified as Amos Paul for allegedly burning the private part of his 10-year-old daughter with a hot pressing iron as punishment for bed-wetting. According to the police, the incident occurred in Unaun, Upenme, in Owo Local Government Area of Ondo St

item.title: Tiger Beer Introduces A Lighter, Smoother Taste For A New Generation Of Beer Drinkers
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Tiger Beer has officially unveiled Tiger 4.0, introducing a refreshed visual identity and a smoother, lighter, and more refreshing taste experience designed for a new generation of consumers. For decades, beer culture has often been defined by intensity — stronger bitterness, heavier taste profiles,

item.title: 2027: IGP Disu reiterates commitment to civil disarmament for peaceful polls
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general elections, the Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Olatunji Rilwan Disu, on Thursday reiterated the commitment of the... The post 2027: IGP Disu reiterates commitment to civil disarmament for peaceful polls appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Akpabio admits he has never seen National Assembly budget
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio has disclosed that he has never seen the National Assembly's budget despite occupying the country's third highest political office, as senators called for greater transparency in the legislature's financial management. The post Akpabio admits he has never seen Natio

item.title: ‘Tinubu is not God’—Jide Kosoko begs Nigerians for patience
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He also claimed that the opposition uses propaganda to control the younger generation, claiming that they are easy to manipulate and control.

item.description: Yobe State Executive Council has approved projects and services worth N73.32 billion for The post Yobe govt approves N73.3bn for agriculture, others appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Senate passes bill increasing fines for 52 traffic offences
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Senate on Thursday passed the Federal Road Safety Corps (Amendment) Bill, 2026, increasing fines for 52 traffic offences as part of efforts to strengthen road safety enforcement across the country. The post Senate passes bill increasing fines for 52 traffic offences appeared first on Tribune Onl

item.title: POWA Convention: IGP Reaffirms Commitment To Police Families’ Welfare

item.title: Why is Pakistan’s Sindh province facing a major child HIV outbreak?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The latest outbreak linked to a Karachi hospital is the newest in a series that experts say reflects systemic failures.

item.title: Kaduna communities begins process for creation of Rigasa LG
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: STAKEHOLDERS from Rigasa and Afaka communities in Kaduna State have begun the process for the creation of a new Rigasa Local Government Area, with its proposed headquarters at Sabon Afaka. The request was submitted to the Igabi Local Government Area by the Standing and Elders Committee for the creat

item.title: Olopade Rallies Team Nigeria for Glasgow 2026 Medal Charge
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Director General of the National Sports Commission (NSC), Hon. Bukola Olopade, has urged Team Nigeria athletes to compete with courage, discipline and patriotism as they prepare to leave their training base in Aberdeen for the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. Addressing the contingent at the end

item.title: 2027: IGP Disu Seeks Intelligence-Driven Offensive Against Arms Proliferation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The inspector-general of police, IGP Olatunji Disu, has unveiled an intelligence-led nationwide offensive against the proliferation of illegal firearms, declaring that the Nigeria Police Force will intensify efforts to recover illicit weapons ahead of future elections. item.title: Abia Govt Partners Private Investors For $145m Solar Manufacturing Plant
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Abia State Government has partnered with private investors to establish a $145 million solar manufacturing plant in the state, as Governor Alex Otti welcomed the investment plan by MD Nwakanma Nigeria Limited and its Chinese technical partners. The governor made this known on Thursday while rece

item.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

item.title: Death at Umahi’s home: Calls for investigation mount
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: More questions and growing calls for a comprehensive and independent investigation have continued to trail the death of 26-year-old Mary Habila at the Ebonyi residence of the Minister of Works, David Umahi. Habila, who her father described as the minister’s personal nurse, reportedly died on June 27

item.title: INEC guidelines: A/Court dashes hope of politicians, parties
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Hopes that political parties could secure more time to conduct their primaries ahead of the 2027 general elections were dashed on Thursday after the Court of Appeal upheld the Independent National Electoral Commission’s (INEC) guidelines for the electoral process. The appellate court, sitting in Abu

item.title: ‘Phantom’ agency DG held at former SARS headquarters
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The controversial Director-General of the Presidential Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, is currently detained at the headquarters of the defunct Special Anti-Robbery Squad (SARS), Daily Trust learnt. It was gathered that Adeyemi, who was flown to Abuja after he was arrested b

item.title: Iron Capital wins Euromoney’s top Nigeria financing award
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Iron Capital has been named Nigeria’s best investment bank for financing solutions at the 2026 Euromoney Awards for Excellence, a read more Iron Capital wins Euromoney’s top Nigeria financing award

item.title: Food prices remain high despite drop in inflation
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Despite the reported drop in headline inflation according to the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS), Nigerians have expressed mixed feelings over the inflation numbers, saying prices of food items have continued to skyrocket while their purchasing powers remain constrained. Those who spoke with Dai

item.title: NBA Insists July 18 Elections Will Hold Despite DSS Arrest
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has insisted that its 2026 National Officers and General Council of the Bar (GCB) elections will go ahead as scheduled on Saturday, July 18, despite the arrest and subsequent release of the Managing Director of its Election Service Provider by the Department of Sta

item.title: BACCODA Hails Gov Mutfwang Over Reinstatement Of Rekna Of Bashar
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Bashar Chiefdom Community Development Association (BACCODA) has commended Plateau State Governor Caleb Mutfwang for reinstating His Royal Highness, Alhaji Abdullahi Idris, as the Rekna of Bashar in Wase Local Government Area. In a statement signed by its president, Dr. Zakari Mohammed, and secre

item.title: Abducted principal, NECO official, 2 candidates rescued in Kogi
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Kogi State Government said security operatives have rescued the four people abducted during Wednesday’s attack on Government Secondary School, Opada-Olowa, in Dekina Local Government Area. Those rescued are the school principal, Elder Daniel Iyanaa; a National Examinations Council (NECO) officia

item.title: Shettima Commissions Abuja City Walk Experience Centre
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima has commissioned the Abuja City Walk Development Sales and Experience Centre, describing the project as part of the Federal Government’s efforts to promote investment and modern urban development in the nation’s capital. Shettima, who represented President

item.title: Prolonged outage triggers outcry in Jigawa LGs
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Residents of Birnin-Kudu, Buji and Gwaram local government areas of Jigawa State have called on relevant authorities to urgently restore electricity supply, following a prolonged blackout that has crippled economic and social activities in the affected communities. The three local government areas i

item.title: US strikes Iran as Tehran warns of Mideast reprisals
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The United States hit Iran on Friday, with Tehran accusing US forces of striking civilians sites and drawing Iranian threats of reprisals on regional infrastructure. The post US strikes Iran as Tehran warns of Mideast reprisals appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: ACPN Urges FG To Integrate Community Pharmacists Into NHIA
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Association of Community Pharmacists of Nigeria (ACPN) has called on President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to immediately integrate registered community pharmacists into the National Health Insurance Authority (NHIA) scheme. The ACPN described the move as a transformative reform capable of expanding acces

item.title: Lupita Nyong’o earns acclaim after ‘The Odyssey’ casting backlash
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I’m very supportive of Chris’s intention with it and with the version of this story that he is telling. Our cast is representative of the world,” she told Elle.

item.title: Kano Hisbah Bans Movie Downloads On Phones To Protect Morality, Islamic Values
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Kano State Hisbah Board has banned the downloading and distribution of movies through mobile phones across all 44 local government areas of the state as part of efforts to protect public morality and uphold Islamic values. The directive, announced on Thursday by the board’s Deputy Commander Gene

item.title: Police bust alleged stolen vehicle deal, recover N7.5m Hilux
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The police chief directed investigators to intensify efforts to track down the fleeing suspect and ensure that everyone connected with the alleged crime is apprehended.

item.title: Sustain the gains of 2026 Hajj

item.description: It must have been a big relief to many that this year’s Hajj exercise to Saudi Arabia ended on a positive note. item.description: The House of Representatives has urged Nigerian youths to play active roles in shaping the country’s democratic future by participating fully in the 2027 general elections. The call was made on Thursday during the Youth Town Hall, one of the flagship events of the 2026 National Assembly Open Week he

item.title: Why Tolanibaj is taking steps to freeze her eggs at 32
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: She explained that her decision comes from the need to keep her options open, adding that she would rather have options than regrets in the future.

item.title: Rotary District 9112 Lays Foundation For N50m Media Centre

item.description: District Governor of Rotary International District 9112, Rotarian Layi Abidoye, has laid the foundation for a N50 million Media Centre at the Rotary Centre, GRA, Ikeja, as part of efforts to strengthen the organisation’s public image and humanitarian outreach. The project, unveiled during the inaugu

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Kogi State Government says the kidnapped school principal, a National Examinations Council (NECO) official, and students writing the ongoing NECO examination at Olowa in Dekina Local Government Area have been rescued. In a statement on Thursday, the Commissioner for Information, Kingsley Femi Fa

item.title: 21 die as school bus crashes in Uganda
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A tragic bus crash in Uganda claims 21 lives, including 20 schoolchildren returning from a trip. Investigations are ongoing into the accident.
Read More: https://punchng.com/21-die-as-school-bus-crashes-in-uganda/

item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have arrested a suspected logistics supplier to Boko Haram/ISWAP terrorists during an intelligence-led operation in Gubio Local Government Area of Borno State. Security expert, Zagazola Makama, disclosed this in a statement posted on X. Zagazola said that the operation

item.title: ADC warns against judicial takeover of political party affairs
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) South-South Zonal Publicity Secretary, Mabel Oboh, has cautioned against allowing the judiciary to take over the affairs of political parties through court pronouncements, saying it could have far-reaching implications for Nigeria’s democracy. item.title: The challenges of protecting Abuja Plan
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: As site officers in charge of districts in the Development Control Department in the late nineties and the onset of the century, we went through lots of tribulations. Some of us survived the system, while others lost their jobs for being good. That was close to 30 years ago. We encountered many chal

item.title: Nine kidnap victims regain freedom after terrorists reportedly fall asleep
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the 17 Brigade have rescued nine kidnap victims who reportedly escaped from a terrorists’ hideout after their captors allegedly fell asleep in Faskari Local Government Area of Katsina State. The development was disclosed in a report by Zagazola Makama, a counter-insurgency and security ana

item.title: World Cup: England’s Anthony Gordon names greatest player of all-time
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England forward, Anthony Gordon, has named Argentina captain, Lionel Messi as the greatest player of all-time. Gordon stated this after England’s World Cup semi-final 2-1 defeat to Argentina on Wednesday. Recall that Gordon’s goal was not enough as Argentina defeated the Three Lions

item.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Lessons from Kano’s health insurance scheme
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Nigeria’s journey toward Universal Health Coverage has often been slowed by familiar barriers: low insurance penetration, high out-of-pocket spending, weak trust in public systems, and the difficulty of reaching poor and vulnerable households before illness pushes them deeper into poverty. Yet Kano

item.title: Ghana’s Airport City is becoming West Africa’s new centre of wealth and power
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: The road to Accra’s airport is becoming the road to Ghana’s economic future. As Airport City booms with hotels, offices and luxury developments, it is redefining where power, wealth and opportunity now reside.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Governor Nasir Idris of Kebbi State has announced that his administration will commence the payment of a N10,000 monthly allowance to National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) members serving in the state. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

item.description:
President Tinubu officially presents VP Kashim Shettima with his APC vice-presidential nomination certificate for the 2027 general election.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-tinubu-presents-shettima-apc-vp-nomination-certificate/

item.title: Tinubu will deliver permanent UN Security Council seat for Nigeria — Jimoh Ibrahim
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to the diplomat, Tinubu has repeatedly used international platforms to advocate for reforms that would guarantee Africa, particularly Nigeria, a permanent place on the council. The post Tinubu will deliver permanent UN Security Council seat for Nigeria — Jimoh Ibrahim appeared first on Van

item.title: FULL LIST: Messi tops golden boot, FIFA power rankings ahead of W’Cup final
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lionel Messi leads the Golden Boot race and FIFA’s Creativity Power Rankings ahead of the 2026 World Cup final. Get the full list of standings here.

item.title: CBN signals no rate cut in sight ahead of MPC meeting
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has signalled that there might be no rush for an easing cycle even though read more CBN signals no rate cut in sight ahead of MPC meeting

item.title: The hidden risks beneath Nigeria’s equity market boom
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Bull markets have a dangerous habit of making almost everyone look like a genius. Nigeria’s equity market has rewarded investors read more The hidden risks beneath Nigeria’s equity market boom

item.title: UBS, Deutsche Bank Raise Stoxx 600 Targets as European Stocks Rally
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Accelerating earnings growth and confidence the rally can withstand the latest geopolitical jitters means strategists are increasingly optimistic about European stocks, a Bloomberg survey shows.

item.title: 65 Battalion Arrests 24 Foreign Nationals In Epe Raid
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army's 65 Battalion have arrested 24 foreign nationals during a raid on a suspected hideout in Epe Local Government Area of Lagos State. The post 65 Battalion Arrests 24 Foreign Nationals In Epe Raid appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Philippine Bourse Seeks to Boost Retail Trading With New Reforms
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The sole stock exchange in the Philippines is preparing a slew of measures including new exchange-traded funds to attract retail investors away from online gambling and cryptocurrency trading.

item.title: Algeria: Fire at Orphanage Kills 11, Including Children
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [DW] A fire at an orphanage in the province of Algiers has killed 11 people and injured 19 more, authorities said. President Abdelmadjid Tebboune expressed his condolences amid his trip to Berlin.

item.title: World Cup stars who could move this summer
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: As the World Cup approaches its end, attentions are turning to the 2026-27 club season - and the players who could be on the move this summer.

item.title: Daughter of migrants, hard liner on migrants: Badenoch’s double standard
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Another UK analysis found that a 1 percent rise in the migrant share of the adult population was associated with roughly a 2 percent increase in GDP per capita and productivity.

item.title: Singer Diamond Platnumz Rportedly Registered All His Assets In His Mums Name
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Smart move!An old video of the singer at his mums birthday announcing that all his assets are in her name has resurfaced amid his ongoing divorce with fellow singer Zuchu.For someone who is reportedly allegedly so serial with women, that is indeed a right move!This simple means that when the divorce

item.title: John Esposito transformed how the West understood Islam
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The pioneering scholar, who died on July 15, challenged Orientalist orthodoxies and reshaped the study of Islam.
